sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255), description TEXT);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, description) VALUES (1, 'Alpha', 'Example with substring 123'), (2, 'Beta', 'Test string with 456'), (3, 'Gamma', NULL), (4, 'Delta', 'Edge case 789');
SELECT id, locate('123', description) < 5 AS found_in_first_five FROM test_table WHERE description IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE locate('Test', description) > 0) < (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table) AS less_than_total FROM test_table LIMIT 1;
SELECT id, locate('Alpha', name) AS position FROM test_table WHERE locate(name, 'A') < 4;
SELECT id, name FROM test_table WHERE locate(name, 'Beta') < (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE locate(456, description) < 2);
SELECT id, locate('123', description) AS position FROM test_table WHERE position IS NOT NULL AND position < 4;
CREATE TABLE another_table (code INT, text_value VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO another_table (code, text_value) VALUES (1, 'Sample 123'), (2, 'Check 456'), (3, NULL);
SELECT a.id, locate(a.name, b.text_value) < 3 FROM test_table a JOIN another_table b ON a.id < b.code WHERE b.text_value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE locate(description, 'Edge case') < 10) AS valid_count FROM test_table GROUP BY id HAVING valid_count > 0;
